# 🎉 9-Wave Production Waves - Complete Restructuring\n\n**Republic development is now organized around 9 Production Waves based on gameplay progression.**\n\nNot infrastructure. Not phases. **Gameplay experience.**\n\nEach Wave builds on the previous. No skipping. No shortcuts.\n\n---\n\n## The 9-Wave Structure\n\n```\nWAVE 0 → WAVE 1 → WAVE 2 → WAVE 3 → WAVE 4 → WAVE 5 → WAVE 6 → WAVE 7 → WAVE 8 → WAVE 9\n  ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓        ↓\nSkeleton Office  Simulation Campaign Government Economy Legislature Military Diplomacy Multiplayer\n```\n\n### Wave 0: Foundation (2-3 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Time Engine, Event Bus, Game Loop, infrastructure\n**What player sees**: Nothing\n**Milestone**: \"The skeleton works\"\n\n### Wave 1: Executive Workspace (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Office, phone, email, visitors, desk, calendar\n**What player sees**: Your office for the first time\n**Milestone**: \"The player has an office\"\n\n### Wave 2: World Simulation (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Countries, currencies, population, resources, government types\n**What player sees**: Reports on a living world\n**Milestone**: \"The world lives without a player\"\n\n### Wave 3: Campaign (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Electoral system, campaigns, elections, parties\n**What player sees**: Campaign interface, opponents, voters\n**Milestone**: \"You can run for office\"\n\n### Wave 4: Government (6-8 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Cabinet, ministers, executive decisions, approval system\n**What player sees**: Cabinet meetings, policy decisions\n**Milestone**: \"You have the power to govern\"\n\n### Wave 5: Economy (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Production, trade, markets, jobs, taxation\n**What player sees**: Economic indicators responding to policy\n**Milestone**: \"Your economy responds to your rule\"\n\n### Wave 6: Legislature (6-8 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Parliament, bills, voting, factions, opposition\n**What player sees**: Parliament chamber, legislation battles\n**Milestone**: \"You must work with parliament\"\n\n### Wave 7: Military (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Armed forces, strategy, warfare, defense\n**What player sees**: Military map, deployments, wars\n**Milestone**: \"You control military power\"\n\n### Wave 8: Diplomacy (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: International relations, treaties, alliances, espionage\n**What player sees**: Other nations, diplomatic negotiations\n**Milestone**: \"You can shape international politics\"\n\n### Wave 9: Multiplayer (4-6 weeks)\n**What gets built**: Networking, multiplayer, human player interaction\n**What player sees**: Other human players as nation leaders\n**Milestone**: \"Politics becomes personal\"\n\n**Total Timeline**: ~61 weeks (14 months)\n\n---\n\n## Why This Order?\n\nEach Wave is a **prerequisite** for the next:\n\n```\nYou can't have Cabinet Meetings\n  without Government\n\nYou can't have Government\n  without Campaign\n\nYou can't have Campaign\n  without Countries\n\nYou can't have Countries\n  without Simulation\n\nYou can't have Simulation\n  without Time\n```\n\n**This is the natural progression of leadership:**\n1. First, you need a world to lead\n2. Then, you need to gain power in that world\n3. Then, you need to govern that world\n4. Then, you need to manage economics\n5. Then, you need to work with parliament\n6. Then, you need military power\n7. Then, you need diplomacy\n8. Then, you compete with other humans\n\n---\n\n## What Changed from Old Structure\n\n### Before (7 Waves - Infrastructure-Based)\n```\nPhase 1: Foundation\nPhase 2: World Building\nPhase 3: Advanced Systems\nPhase 4: Intelligence\nPhase 5: Multiplayer\nPhase 6: Presentation\n```\n\nThese were about **building layers** of technology.\n\n### After (9 Waves - Gameplay-Based)\n```\nWave 0: Foundation\nWave 1: Executive Workspace ← Player enters the game\nWave 2: World Simulation\nWave 3: Campaign ← Player gains power\nWave 4: Government ← Player wields power\nWave 5: Economy ← Player sees consequences\nWave 6: Legislature ← Player faces opposition\nWave 7: Military ← Player has ultimate power\nWave 8: Diplomacy ← Player multiplies power\nWave 9: Multiplayer ← Player faces humans\n```\n\nThese are about **what the player experiences**.\n\n---\n\n## Documents Created/Updated\n\n### New Documents\n✅ **[.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)**\n- Complete 9-Wave strategy\n- Detailed breakdown of each Wave\n- Why this order matters\n- The experience arc\n- ~18,500 words\n\n### Updated Documents\n✅ **[Docs/Roadmap/README.md](./Docs/Roadmap/README.md)**\n- Timeline for all 9 Waves\n- What player sees at each Wave\n- Milestone gates for each Wave\n- Dependency chain\n\n✅ **[Docs/Features/README.md](./Docs/Features/README.md)**\n- Features organized by 9 Waves\n- Systems to build in each Wave\n- Player experience for each Wave\n- Republic Test for features\n\n✅ **[Docs/README.md](./Docs/README.md)**\n- Updated to reference 9 Waves\n- Updated links to new strategy\n- Emphasized Production Waves\n\n---\n\n## Wave Timeline\n\n| Wave | Name | Duration | Start | End | Status |\n|------|------|----------|-------|-----|--------|\n| 0 | Foundation | 2-3 wks | Week 1 | Week 3 | 🟡 In Progress |\n| 1 | Executive Workspace | 4-6 wks | Week 4 | Week 9 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 2 | World Simulation | 4-6 wks | Week 10 | Week 15 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 3 | Campaign | 4-6 wks | Week 16 | Week 21 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 4 | Government | 6-8 wks | Week 22 | Week 29 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 5 | Economy | 4-6 wks | Week 30 | Week 35 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 6 | Legislature | 6-8 wks | Week 36 | Week 43 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 7 | Military | 4-6 wks | Week 44 | Week 49 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 8 | Diplomacy | 4-6 wks | Week 50 | Week 55 | 🔴 Planned |\n| 9 | Multiplayer | 4-6 wks | Week 56 | Week 61 | 🔴 Planned |\n| **Total** | **~61 weeks** | **43-61 wks** | **Week 1** | **Week 61** | **On Track** |\n\n---\n\n## The Player's Journey\n\n### Wave 0\n\"I see infrastructure being built.\"\n\n### Wave 1\n\"I sit at my desk in my office.\"\n\n### Wave 2\n\"I learn about my country. It's a living world.\"\n\n### Wave 3\n\"I fight to become the leader.\"\n\n### Wave 4\n\"I'm the leader. I make decisions that affect millions.\"\n\n### Wave 5\n\"I see my policies ripple through the economy. People prosper or suffer based on my choices.\"\n\n### Wave 6\n\"I battle in parliament. I can't just decree things. I have to convince others.\"\n\n### Wave 7\n\"I send soldiers to war. People die. Wars reshape nations.\"\n\n### Wave 8\n\"I negotiate with other nations. I form alliances. I gain power through diplomacy.\"\n\n### Wave 9\n\"I play against real humans. Betrayal matters. Trust is valuable. The intrigue is real.\"\n\n**The burden of leadership grows with each Wave.**\n\n---\n\n## Key Principles\n\n### 1. No Skipping\nYou cannot skip a Wave. Each Wave is a prerequisite for the next.\n\n### 2. No Shortcuts\nEach Wave must be **complete** before starting the next. No partial implementations.\n\n### 3. Natural Progression\nEach Wave builds naturally on the previous. Systems from earlier Waves enable later Waves.\n\n### 4. Player-Centric\nWaves are defined by **what the player experiences**, not by infrastructure layers.\n\n### 5. Each Wave Ships\nEach Wave is a complete, playable experience that stands on its own (but works better with later Waves).\n\n---\n\n## How to Use This\n\n### For Developers\n1. Read [.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n2. Check current Wave status in [Docs/Roadmap/README.md](./Docs/Roadmap/README.md)\n3. Look at systems to build in [Docs/Features/README.md](./Docs/Features/README.md)\n4. Find issues labeled with current Wave\n5. Build!\n\n### For Designers\n1. Read [.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n2. Review [Design Principles](./Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)\n3. Contribute design docs for current Wave\n4. Plan next Wave features\n\n### For Project Managers\n1. Read [.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)\n2. Use [Docs/Roadmap/README.md](./Docs/Roadmap/README.md) timeline\n3. Track Wave completion gates\n4. Monitor velocity\n5. Plan next Wave when current Wave nears completion\n\n### For Stakeholders\n1. Read [.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md) overview section\n2. Track Wave milestones\n3. See what players will experience at each Wave\n4. Plan announcements/communications around Wave gates\n\n---\n\n## The North Star\n\n> **Republic is not a game about ruling people.**\n>\n> **It is a game about carrying the burden of democratic leadership.**\n\nEach Wave reinforces this:\n\n- **Wave 1** (Office): You feel the isolation of leadership\n- **Wave 2** (Simulation): You realize the world is complex\n- **Wave 3** (Campaign): You learn that power must be earned\n- **Wave 4** (Government): You feel the weight of decisions\n- **Wave 5** (Economy): You see that choices have consequences\n- **Wave 6** (Legislature): You learn that power is limited\n- **Wave 7** (Military): You understand that force has costs\n- **Wave 8** (Diplomacy): You see that power can be multiplied\n- **Wave 9** (Multiplayer): You discover that trust is political currency\n\n---\n\n## Success Checklist\n\n**Republic Studio is working when:**\n\n✅ All 9 Waves documented and understood by team\n✅ Wave 0 in progress\n✅ Wave 1 systems defined\n✅ GitHub boards set up per Wave\n✅ Issues labeled by Wave\n✅ Team moving issues across columns daily\n✅ Velocity tracked\n✅ Wave dependencies clear\n✅ Milestone gates defined\n✅ Release strategy clear\n\n---\n\n## Key Documents\n\n### Strategy\n- **[.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md](./.github/PRODUCTION_WAVES.md)** - Complete 9-Wave strategy\n- **[Republic Manifesto](./Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/MANIFESTO.md)** - The north star\n- **[Design Principles](./Docs/Gameplay%20Philosophy/Design%20Principles.md)** - 16 core principles\n\n### Development\n- **[Docs/Roadmap/README.md](./Docs/Roadmap/README.md)** - Timeline and progress\n- **[Docs/Features/README.md](./Docs/Features/README.md)** - What to build per Wave\n- **[Docs/Architecture/README.md](./Docs/Architecture/README.md)** - How to build it\n\n### GitHub\n- **[.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO.md)** - How it all works\n- **[.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md](./.github/REPUBLIC_STUDIO_SETUP.md)** - GitHub setup\n- **[.github/README.md](./.github/README.md)** - Navigation\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. ✅ Read all Wave documentation\n2. ✅ Share with team\n3. ✅ Discuss Wave strategy\n4. ✅ Set up GitHub per Wave structure\n5. ✅ Begin Wave 0 (if not already started)\n6. ✅ Complete Wave 0 by Week 3\n7. ✅ Begin Wave 1 at Week 4\n8. ✅ Complete all 9 Waves by Week 61\n9. ✅ Ship Republic 🚀\n\n---\n\n## The Journey\n\n```\nWave 0: Foundation\n   ↓ (2-3 weeks)\nWave 1: Executive Workspace\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 2: World Simulation\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 3: Campaign\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 4: Government\n   ↓ (6-8 weeks)\nWave 5: Economy\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 6: Legislature\n   ↓ (6-8 weeks)\nWave 7: Military\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 8: Diplomacy\n   ↓ (4-6 weeks)\nWave 9: Multiplayer\n   ↓\n🎉 RELEASE - The burden of leadership, fully realized\n```\n\n**~61 weeks. 9 Waves. One complete game.**\n\n---\n\n*Republic: From skeleton to office to world to power to consequences to opposition to military to diplomacy to multiplayer. Each Wave builds on the last. No skipping. No shortcuts. Build it right.*\n"
